This update in Manis AI just changed how you use Google Workspace forever. I'm talking Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive, Gmail, all of it. One prompt, one agent, everything done. No more switching tabs, no more copy paste, no more wasted hours on work that should take 2 minutes. Manis can now literally control your entire workspace from a single instruction. And when you combine it with their desktop feature, your local files and your cloud tools are connected to. This is not another AI chat tool. This is an AI operator. It doesn't just suggest, it executes. And the people who understand this right now are going to be miles ahead of everyone else. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. He genuinely wants to know what you think about this one. All right, let's get into it. Most people work like this right now. You open Google Docs, you write something, you need a number, so you switch to Sheets, you need a file, so you go to Drive, you need to build a deck, so you open Slides, and you're bouncing between five different tabs just to complete one task. Every switch costs you time. Every switch costs you focus. And by the end of the day, you've spent more time managing tools than actually doing the work that matters. Now, here's the thing. AI was supposed to fix this. But if we're honest, it only solved half the problem. AI got really good at thinking. It could write for you. It could summarize. It could generate ideas and give you frameworks. But you still had to go and do the actual work yourself. You still had to open the tools, move the data, update the spreadsheet, and organize the files. That's the gap that's been frustrating everyone. AI with brains but no hands. Smart enough to know what needs to happen but not able to actually make it happen inside the systems you use every day. That gap just closed with this Manis update and it's a bigger deal than most people are realizing. Manis AI has integrated with the Google Workspace CLI. CLI stands for command line interface. Don't worry about the technical side of that. Here's what it means in plain English. Manus can now directly control your Google Workspace. Not just read it, not just analyze it, actually control it, edit it, execute inside it in real time. We're talking Google Docs, Google Sheets, Google Slides, Drive, Gmail, and Calendar. All of it unified under one AI agent that takes your instructions and acts on them inside your real files. You type a prompt, Manis does the work in your actual workspace. Right now, before this update, if you wanted to automate something across your workspace, you needed multiple APIs, integrations, scripts, and usually a developer to stitch it all together. That was the barrier. It was expensive, slow, and complicated. Now, you just talk to Manis and it executes. The CLI acts as a single unified layer that connects all the workspace tools and feeds structured data directly to the AI so it can take action. It even has built-in agent skills specifically designed for this kind of automation. That's the shift from AI as a thinking tool to AI as an execution layer. Quick pause here. Let me show you exactly what this looks like in practice. And I'm going to use real examples that apply to running and growing a community like the AI profit boardroom because this is the point. This is not abstract theory. This is what AI automation actually looks like in a real business that's operating right now. Example one. Every week you want to send a community update to your AI profit boardroom members. You need to pull the latest engagement data from your tracking spreadsheet. Write a clean summary doc. Format it properly and save it to the right folder in drive so your team can access it. Old way, open the spreadsheet, find all the numbers, write the doc manually, format it, save it to the right place. That's an hour of work minimum every single week. New way with Manis one prompt something like pull the latest engagement numbers from the tracking sheet. Write a weekly update dock for AI profit boardroom members and save it to the community updates folder in Drive. Manis does all of it. You review it, approve it, and send 15 minutes instead of an hour. Example two, new members are joining the AI profit boardroom every week. You want each of them to receive a personalized welcome document with their name, their membership tier, and the specific resources that are most relevant to them. Old way, you'd be manually copying names into templates one by one. It doesn't scale. It takes forever. And it's the kind of repetitive work that burns people out. New way with Manis. One prompt. Generate personalized onboarding documents for every new member who joined this week. Pull their names and membership tiers from the onboarding sheet. Save each one to drive. Done automatically, whether it's five new members or 500. Example three. You want to create a pitch deck to bring in new brand partners or sponsors for the AI profit boardroom. You need community growth stats, key value points, and a professionallook presentation. One prompt to manice using our growth data from the stats spreadsheet. Build a Google slides deck that showcases the AI profit boardroom community highlights our member engagement and explains why brands should work with us. Manis pulls the data, builds the deck, formats the slides. You walk into that partner meeting with a proper presentation and you didn't spend a single hour building it yourself. That is what execution layer AI looks like. Not suggestions, actions inside your real tools right now. Here's what I want you to understand. This update is not just a cool new Google integration. This is a fundamental shift in what AI actually is and what it can do. For years, AI lived in the thinking layer. You gave it information. It gave you output, a draft, a summary, a plan. Then you had to go and execute everything yourself. AI was smart but passive. It could advise you on the play, but it couldn't run onto the field and make it happen. This update moves Manis into the execution layer. It doesn't just tell you what to do. It does it inside the systems you already use every day. That's the line that just got crossed.
